Name: 2p-revenue-optimizer-api
section: Spring Boot App model for Java 17
content: The properties of \*\*application\.yml\*\* are always loaded and at the same time they are complemented with \*\*application\-<SCOPE\_SUFFIX\>\.yml\*\* properties\. If a property is in both files, the one that is configured in \*\*application\-<SCOPE\_SUFFIX\>\.yml\*\* has preference over the property of \*\*application\.yml\*\*\.

section: Spring Boot App model for Java 17
content: For example, for the \*\*SCOPE\*\* \'items\-loader\-test\' the \*\*SCOPE\_SUFFIX\*\* would be \'test\' and the loaded property files will be \*\*application\.yml\*\* and \*\*application\-test\.yml\*\*

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: A middleware service that handles payment\-related self\-service operations for GreatMart\'s payment system\. This service acts as an intermediary layer between the frontend applications and the core payment services\.

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid graph TD     subgraph Frontend         UI\[Frontend Applications\]     end      subgraph Payment Promise Gateway         API\[REST API Layer\]         CON\[Controllers\]         SER\[Services\]         CMD\[Commands\]         BO\[Business Objects\]         UTL\[Utils\]     end      subgraph External Services         CRE\[Core Payment Services\]         PRO\[Promise Service\]         MET\[Metrics Service\]     end      UI \-\-\> API     API \-\-\> CON     CON \-\-\> SER     SER \-\-\> CMD     SER \-\-\> BO     SER \-\-\> UTL     SER \-\-\> CRE     SER \-\-\> PRO     SER \-\-\> MET </code\_tkn\>

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: \- \*\*Language\*\*: Java 17 \- \*\*Framework\*\*: Spring Boot 3\.2\.4 \- \*\*Build Tool\*\*: Gradle \- \*\*Dependency Injection\*\*: Google Guice \- \*\*Documentation\*\*: OpenAPI/Swagger \- \*\*Monitoring\*\*: DataDog Metrics

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: <code\_tkn\> src/main/java/com/greatmart/payments/selfservice/ ├── config/          \# Configuration classes ├── controller/      \# REST controllers ├── entities/        \# Domain entities │   ├── bo/         \# Business Objects │   ├── commands/   \# Command objects │   └── workflowconfig/ \# WorkflowCloud configuration ├── exceptions/      \# Custom exceptions ├── metrics/         \# Metrics collectors ├── services/        \# Business logic └── utils/          \# Utility classes </code\_tkn\>

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 1\. \*\*Command Pattern\*\*    \- Used for encapsulating request parameters    \- Examples: \`PromiseCommand\`, \`LateFeeForgivenessCommand\`

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 2\. \*\*Builder Pattern\*\*    \- Used in Business Objects construction    \- Examples: \`ActivePromiseBO\.Builder\`

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 3\. \*\*Dependency Injection\*\*    \- Using Google Guice for dependency management    \- Spring\'s \`@Autowired\` and Guice\'s \`@Inject\`

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 4\. \*\*Factory Pattern\*\*    \- Used in service creation and configuration

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 5\. \*\*DTO Pattern\*\*    \- Used for data transfer between layers    \- Examples: \`LateFeeForgivenessDTO\`, \`UserDebtDTO\`

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: \- Retrieves the offers or remedies available to the user\. Shows the different options the user has to regularize their debt\.

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: The API is documented using OpenAPI/Swagger\. Main endpoints:

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 1\. \`GET /payment\_promise/form\` 2\. \`GET /payment\_promise/active\` 3\. \`GET /offers\` 4\. \`POST /payment\_promise\` 5\. \`POST /forgiveness\_proposal\` 6\. \`PUT /forgiveness\_proposal\` 7\. \`GET /forgiveness\_proposal/\{promiseId\}\`

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: Detailed API documentation can be found in \[specs\-hub\]\(https://web\.workflowcloud\.io/payment\-promise\-gateway/specs\-hub\)\.

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: This section provides sequence diagrams for each API endpoint, showing the interaction flow between system components\.

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 1\. \*\*GET /payment\_promise/form\*\*    \- Used to obtain available options for the payment promise form    \- Requires user identification and site identification    \- Useful for the initial configuration of the promise form

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 2\. \*\*GET /payment\_promise/active\*\*    \- Queries the user\'s active payment promise    \- Only requires user identification    \- Allows verifying if the user has an active promise

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 3\. \*\*GET /offers\*\*    \- Retrieves offers or remedies available to the user    \- Considers user ID, site, and user type    \- Allows displaying customized options according to the user\'s profile

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 4\. \*\*POST /payment\_promise\*\*    \- Creates a new payment promise    \- Requires a PromiseCommand object with promise details    \- Validates the data before creating the promise

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 5\. \*\*POST /forgiveness\_proposal\*\*    \- Creates a late fee forgiveness proposal    \- Uses LateFeeForgivenessPromiseCommand for details    \- Allows initiating the forgiveness process

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 6\. \*\*PUT /forgiveness\_proposal\*\*    \- Confirms an existing forgiveness proposal    \- Requires LateFeeForgivenessProposalCommand for confirmation    \- Finalizes the forgiveness process

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: 7\. \*\*GET /forgiveness\_proposal/\{promiseId\}\*\*    \- Queries the details of a specific forgiveness    \- Validates that the promise ID is numeric    \- Returns detailed information about the forgiveness in DTO format

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: Below are examples of how to interact with the API using curl commands:

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: <code\_tkn\>bash \#\!/bin/bash  \# Common variables BASE\_URL\="http://localhost:8080/payments/self\-service" AUTH\_TOKEN\="your\-bearer\-token"  \# 1\. GET /payment\_promise/form \# Gets the form options for payment promises curl \-X GET "$\{BASE\_URL\}/payment\_promise/form" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "caller\.id\=24517" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "site\_id\=nta"  \# 2\. GET /payment\_promise/active \# Gets the user\'s active payment promise curl \-X GET "$\{BASE\_URL\}/payment\_promise/active" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-H "x\-caller\-id: 24517" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "caller\.id\=24517"  \# 3\. GET /offers \# Gets available offers for the user curl \-X GET "$\{BASE\_URL\}/offers" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "caller\.id\=24517" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "site\_id\=ntb" \\   \-\-data\-urlencode "vertical\=consumer"  \# 4\. POST /payment\_promise \# Creates a new payment promise curl \-X POST "$\{BASE\_URL\}/payment\_promise" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-d \'\{     "borrowerId": 987654321,     "amount": 1000\.50,     "siteId": "NTA",     "paymentMethod": "CREDIT\_CARD",     "dateCreated": "2025\-05\-12T15:54:43\-05:00",     "datePaid": "2025\-05\-15T15:54:43\-05:00",     "originEntity": "SELF\_SERVICE"   \}\'  \# 5\. POST /forgiveness\_proposal \# Creates a late fee forgiveness proposal curl \-X POST "$\{BASE\_URL\}/forgiveness\_proposal" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-d \'\{     "borrowerId": 987654321,     "amount": 500\.75,     "siteId": "NTA"   \}\'  \# 6\. PUT /forgiveness\_proposal \# Confirms a late fee forgiveness proposal curl \-X PUT "$\{BASE\_URL\}/forgiveness\_proposal" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" \\   \-d \'\{     "borrowerId": 987654321,     "proposalId": 123456789,     "siteId": "NTA"   \}\'  \# 7\. GET /forgiveness\_proposal/\{promiseId\} \# Gets the late fee forgiveness information for a specific promise curl \-X GET "$\{BASE\_URL\}/forgiveness\_proposal/7741596914" \\   \-H "Authorization: Bearer $\{AUTH\_TOKEN\}" \\   \-H "Content\-Type: application/json" </code\_tkn\>

Name: payment-promise-gateway
content: A middleware service that handles payment\-related self\-service operations for GreatMart\'s payment system\. This service acts as an intermediary layer between the frontend applications and the core payment services\.

content: Comprehensive guide to the Catalog Portfolio API service architecture, system design, and component interactions\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: The Catalog Portfolio API service is a RESTful microservice built with Java and Spark framework, designed to manage brand information in RetailFlow\'s Catalog platform\. It provides centralized brand management for the curated portfolio with high availability and scalability\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: This section provides a comprehensive overview of the system architecture and design principles\.

content: <i class\="fas fa\-download"\></i\> Download Architecture Template

content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid %%\{init: \{   \'theme\': \'base\',   \'themeVariables\': \{     \'primaryColor\': \'\#ADD8E6\',     \'primaryTextColor\': \'\#000080\',     \'primaryBorderColor\': \'\#000\',     \'lineColor\': \'\#ff9800\',     \'secondaryColor\': \'\#006064\',     \'tertiaryColor\': \'\#fff\',     \'noteTextColor\': \'\#333\',     \'textColor\': \'\#fff\'   \} \}\}%% graph TB     Client\[Client Applications\] \-\-\> LB\[Load Balancer\]     LB \-\-\> API\[Catalog Portfolio API\]          API \-\-\> Controller\[Controllers Layer\]     Controller \-\-\> Service\[Service Layer\]     Service \-\-\> DAO\[DAO Layer\]     DAO \-\-\> DB\[\(MySQL Database\)\]          Service \-\-\> PicturesAPI\[Pictures API\]     Service \-\-\> RegulationsAPI\[Regulations API\]     Service \-\-\> BigQ\[BigQuery Events\]          API \-\-\> Metrics\[Metrics Collection\]     Metrics \-\-\> DataDog\[DataDog Dashboard\]          Controller \-\-\> BrandController\[Brand Controller\]     Controller \-\-\> LogoController\[Logo Controller\]     Controller \-\-\> CollectorController\[Collector Controller\]     Controller \-\-\> ResourceController\[Resource Controller\]          Service \-\-\> BrandService\[Brand Service\]     Service \-\-\> LogoService\[Logo Service\]     Service \-\-\> CollectorService\[Collector Service\]     Service \-\-\> ResourceService\[Resource Service\]          DAO \-\-\> BrandDAO\[Brand DAO\]     DAO \-\-\> LogoDAO\[Logo DAO\]     DAO \-\-\> CollectorDAO\[Collector DAO\]     DAO \-\-\> ResourceDAO\[Resource DAO\] </code\_tkn\>

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Brands can exist in three possible states:

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid %%\{init: \{   \'theme\': \'base\',   \'themeVariables\': \{     \'primaryColor\': \'\#ADD8E6\',     \'primaryTextColor\': \'\#000080\',     \'primaryBorderColor\': \'\#000\',     \'lineColor\': \'\#ff9800\',     \'secondaryColor\': \'\#006064\',     \'tertiaryColor\': \'\#fff\',     \'noteTextColor\': \'\#333\',     \'textColor\': \'\#fff\'   \} \}\}%% graph TD     A\[New Brand\] \-\-\> B\[STAND\_BY\]     B \-\-\> C\[ACTIVE\]     C \-\-\> B     B \-\-\> D\[DELETED\]     C \-\-\> D          B \-\-\> E\[Logo Required\]     E \-\-\> B          style A fill:\#ffc66d     style B fill:\#6897bb     style C fill:\#4CAF50     style D fill:\#f44336 </code\_tkn\>

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \- \*\*STAND\_BY\*\*: Brand is created but not yet active\. May be missing required logos or temporarily deactivated\. \- \*\*ACTIVE\*\*: Brand is fully functional and visible in the system\. All requirements are met\. \- \*\*DELETED\*\*: Brand has been permanently removed \(irreversible action\)\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Before activation, brands must pass these validations: \- Main logo uploaded and processed \- Valid category and site combination \- Compliance with regulations service requirements \- All required fields populated

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Common errors and their meanings: \- \`brand\_not\_found\`: Brand ID does not exist \- \`logos\_missing\`: Required logos not uploaded \- \`validation\_failed\`: Business rules validation failed \- \`unauthorized\`: Missing permissions for the operation

Name: catalog-portfolio-api

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <code\_tkn\>bash \# Check if configuration files exist ls \-la src/main/resources/development/ ls \-la src/main/resources/production/  \# Validate properties format grep \-v \'^\#\' src/main/resources/development/application\.properties </code\_tkn\>

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Search for brands with various filters and pagination\.

content: \| Parameter \| Type \| Required \| Description \|

content: \| \`collector\_id\` \| integer \| No \| Filter by collector ID \|

content: \| \`brand\_name\` \| string \| No \| Filter by brand name \|

content: \| \`site\` \| string \| No \| Filter by site \(MLA, MLB, MLC, etc\.\) \|

content: \| \`tag\` \| string \| No \| Filter by tag \(can be used multiple times\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`owner\_id\` \| integer \| No \| Filter by owner ID \|

content: \| \`type\` \| string \| No \| Filter by type \(lighthouse, standard\) \|

content: \| \`status\` \| string \| No \| Filter by status \(ACTIVE, STAND\_BY\) \|

content: \| \`offset\` \| integer \| No \| Number of items to skip \(default: 0\) \|

content: \| \`limit\` \| integer \| No \| Number of items to return \(default: 50, max: 200\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Comprehensive guide to the Catalog Portfolio API service architecture, system design, and component interactions\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: The Catalog Portfolio API service is a RESTful microservice built with Java and Spark framework, designed to manage brand information in RetailFlow\'s Catalog platform\. It provides centralized brand management for the curated portfolio with high availability and scalability\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: This section provides a comprehensive overview of the system architecture and design principles\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <i class\="fas fa\-download"\></i\> Download Architecture Template

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \- \[Brand Lifecycle\]\(lifecycle\.md\) \- Complete brand state management and transitions \- \[Creating Brands\]\(creation\.md\) \- Step\-by\-step brand creation process \- \[Brand Updates\]\(updates\.md\) \- Updating existing brand information \- \[Brand Activation\]\(activation\.md\) \- Brand activation process and requirements \- \[Brand Validation\]\(validation\.md\) \- Validation rules and business logic

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <i class\="fas fa\-download"\></i\> Download Brand Model Template

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: A brand represents a merchant or company in the curated portfolio with the following structure:

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <code\_tkn\>json \{     "id": 2,     "name": "McDonald\'s",     "soft\_descriptor": "MCDONALDS",     "site": "MLB",     "category": 5611201,     "subcategories": \[\],     "type": "lighthouse",     "tags": \[         "611724878",         "bf\_mlb\_2020"     \],     "logos": \[         \{             "id": 1290,             "brand\_id": 2,             "type": "cover",             "picture\_id": "620688\-MLA45741117352\_042021"         \},         \{             "id": 23,             "brand\_id": 2,             "type": "main",             "picture\_id": "979677\-MLA43159708639\_082020"         \}     \],     "resources": \[         \{             "type": "card",             "url": "https://http2\.retailflow\-static\.com/storage/api\-instore\-resource/brand\-041299f0\-a3e0\-4faa\-a41f\-8dd06784b5e3\-card\.png"         \},         \{             "type": "pin",             "url": "https://http2\.retailflow\-static\.com/storage/api\-instore\-resource/brand\-041299f0\-a3e0\-4faa\-a41f\-8dd06784b5e3\-pin\.png"         \}     \],     "priority": 9,     "status": "ACTIVE",     "date\_created": "2019\-11\-25T17:17:24\.000\-04:00",     "date\_last\_updated": "2021\-06\-10T13:17:10\.000\-04:00" \} </code\_tkn\>

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Brands can exist in three possible states:

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid %%\{init: \{   \'theme\': \'base\',   \'themeVariables\': \{     \'primaryColor\': \'\#ADD8E6\',     \'primaryTextColor\': \'\#000080\',     \'primaryBorderColor\': \'\#000\',     \'lineColor\': \'\#ff9800\',     \'secondaryColor\': \'\#006064\',     \'tertiaryColor\': \'\#fff\',     \'noteTextColor\': \'\#333\',     \'textColor\': \'\#fff\'   \} \}\}%% graph TD     A\[New Brand\] \-\-\> B\[STAND\_BY\]     B \-\-\> C\[ACTIVE\]     C \-\-\> B     B \-\-\> D\[DELETED\]     C \-\-\> D          B \-\-\> E\[Logo Required\]     E \-\-\> B          style A fill:\#ffc66d     style B fill:\#6897bb     style C fill:\#4CAF50     style D fill:\#f44336 </code\_tkn\>

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \- \*\*STAND\_BY\*\*: Brand is created but not yet active\. May be missing required logos or temporarily deactivated\. \- \*\*ACTIVE\*\*: Brand is fully functional and visible in the system\. All requirements are met\. \- \*\*DELETED\*\*: Brand has been permanently removed \(irreversible action\)\.

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Before activation, brands must pass these validations: \- Main logo uploaded and processed \- Valid category and site combination \- Compliance with regulations service requirements \- All required fields populated

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: Common errors and their meanings: \- \`brand\_not\_found\`: Brand ID does not exist \- \`logos\_missing\`: Required logos not uploaded \- \`validation\_failed\`: Business rules validation failed \- \`unauthorized\`: Missing permissions for the operation

content: Search for brands with various filters and pagination\.

content: \| Parameter \| Type \| Required \| Description \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`collector\_id\` \| integer \| No \| Filter by collector ID \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`brand\_name\` \| string \| No \| Filter by brand name \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`site\` \| string \| No \| Filter by site \(MLA, MLB, MLC, etc\.\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`tag\` \| string \| No \| Filter by tag \(can be used multiple times\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`owner\_id\` \| integer \| No \| Filter by owner ID \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`type\` \| string \| No \| Filter by type \(lighthouse, standard\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`status\` \| string \| No \| Filter by status \(ACTIVE, STAND\_BY\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`offset\` \| integer \| No \| Number of items to skip \(default: 0\) \|

Name: catalog-portfolio-api
content: \| \`limit\` \| integer \| No \| Number of items to return \(default: 50, max: 200\) \|

content: <code\_tkn\>bash curl "https://api\.mp\.internal\.retailflow\.com/catalog/brands/?site\=MLA&status\=ACTIVE&limit\=10" </code\_tkn\>

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: Mediante un PR a este \[mismo repositorio\]\(https://github\.com/globalpay/unified\-transaction\-configs\) respetando la estructura de directior planteada\.

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: Esta estructura es:

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: \- Ambiente \(\`development\|production\`\) \- Tipo de archivo \(\`flow\|reader\`\) \- Sistema operativo \(\`android\|ios\|etc\`\) \- PoiType \(sólo para \`smartpos\|standalone\`\) \- Versión \(\`0\.0\.0\|1\.123\.1\.1\`\) \(\*\*SIEMPRE\*\* deb estar presente una versión por default 0\.0\.0 o 0\.0\.0\.0 según SO, respetando semver de 4 dígitos máximo\) \- Site \(\`GPA\|GPB\|GPM\|GPC\`\) \- Grupo de usuario \(\*\*SIEMPRE\*\* debe estar presente una carpeta default con una configuración default\) \- Archivo de configuración con el nombre \`config\.json\`

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: Se crea la carpeta en el lugar correspondiente \(\`$AMBIENTE/$TIPO\_ARCHIVO/$SO/$POITYPE/$VERSION/$SITE/$GRUPO\_USUARIOS\`\) el cual contendrá un

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: archivo con el nombre

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: \`users\.json\` con el siguiente cuerpo:

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: <code\_tkn\>json \{   "users": \[     1,     2,     3,     \.\.\.,     n   \] \} </code\_tkn\>

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: \- El JSON no debe pesar más de 50kb, esta limitación es por KVS \- Que el JSON sea válido \- Que las versiones respeten semver de máximo 4 dígitos \- Que exista siempre una versión default 0\.0\.0 o 0\.0\.0\.0 con un grupo de usuario default y una configuración default \- Que se respete la estructura del directorio propuesto

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: Para que una actualización impacte es necesario:

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: \- Hacer un merge a master en el repositorio de \[unified transaction configs\]\(https://github\.com/globalpay/unified\-transaction\-configs\) \- Realizar el PUT correspondiente según \[la documentación de transaction flow orchestrator\]\(http://globalpaydocs\.io/transaction\-flow\-orchestrator/\)

Name: unified-transaction-configs
content: El fin de este proyecto es la carga de las distintas configuraciones de los distintos flujos\.

Name: order-workflow-api
order-workflow-api
content: Workflow API simplifies and standardizes the development flow of applications integrated into Release Process by performing the following tasks: \* Validate the branching model \(see the related section in \[Release Process\]\(http://retailcorp\-docs\.io/release\-process/guide/\)\) \* Protect the important branches setting:     \* Minimum approvers     \* Required checks \* Create backports to help keeping the branches in sync\. \(e\.g\.: from master to develop after merging a PR\) \* Create automatic versions based on a cron\-like configuration\.

Name: order-workflow-api
content: A MySQL database is used to store the following information:

Name: order-workflow-api
content: \* Workflow configuration \(protected branches, releasable branches, etc\.\) \* Automatic version creation schedule\.

Name: order-workflow-api
content: For more information see \[Database\]\(/services/database\.md\)

Name: order-workflow-api
content: A BigQ Consumer is used to receive tasks from the \[Orchestrator\]\(http://retailcorp\-docs\.io/order\-orchestrator\-api/guide/\)

Name: order-workflow-api
content: and webhooks from \[EventCollector\]\(https://retailcorp\-docs\.io/workflow\-collector/0\.1\.3\-rc\-1/guide/\#/usage\)

Name: order-workflow-api
content: For more information see \[BigQ\]\(/services/bigq\.md\)

Name: order-workflow-api
content: The Jobs service is used to automatically create versions based on a cron\-like scheduler\.

Name: order-workflow-api
content: For more information see \[Jobs\]\(/services/jobs\.md\)

content: \|  Type \| Schema \| Cluster \|

Name: order-workflow-api
content: \| MySQL \| fulfillment \| orderworkflowapi00 \|

Name: order-workflow-api
content: A workflow represents a valid git flow in use for Release process\.

Name: order-workflow-api
content: Currently we have two types:

Name: order-workflow-api
content: \- Gitflow \- Libflow

Name: order-workflow-api
content: A Workflow object is represented as a JSON as follows:

Name: order-workflow-api
content: <code\_tkn\> \{     &\#34;default\_branch&\#34;: &\#34;develop&\#34;,     &\#34;name&\#34;: &\#34;gitflow&\#34;,     &\#34;documentation\_link&\#34;: &\#34;https://release\-process\.retailcorp\-cloud\.io/\#/lang\-es/workflows/02\_gitflow?id\=branches\-destacados&\#34;,     &\#34;stable\_branches&\#34;: \[         \{             &\#34;name&\#34;: &\#34;master&\#34;,             &\#34;name\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^master$&\#34;,             &\#34;code\_review\_enabled&\#34;: false,             &\#34;accept\_forks&\#34;: false,             &\#34;valid\_head\_branch\_patterns&\#34;: \[                 &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^hotfix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^revert\-\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^migration/\.\*&\#34;             \],             &\#34;releasable&\#34;: true,             &\#34;candidate&\#34;: false         \},         \{             &\#34;name&\#34;: &\#34;develop&\#34;,             &\#34;name\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^develop$&\#34;,             &\#34;code\_review\_enabled&\#34;: true,             &\#34;accept\_forks&\#34;: true,             &\#34;valid\_head\_branch\_patterns&\#34;: \[                 &\#34;^feature/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^enhancement/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^bugfix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^fix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^revert\-\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^hotfix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^backport/\.\*&\#34;             \],             &\#34;releasable&\#34;: false,             &\#34;candidate&\#34;: false         \},         \{             &\#34;name&\#34;: &\#34;release&\#34;,             &\#34;name\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,             &\#34;code\_review\_enabled&\#34;: true,             &\#34;accept\_forks&\#34;: false,             &\#34;valid\_head\_branch\_patterns&\#34;: \[                 &\#34;^fix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^revert\-\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^hotfix/\.\*&\#34;,                 &\#34;^backport/\.\*&\#34;             \],             &\#34;releasable&\#34;: false,             &\#34;candidate&\#34;: true         \}     \],     &\#34;relevant\_branches&\#34;: \[         &\#34;^master$&\#34;,         &\#34;^develop$&\#34;,         &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^hotfix/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^feature/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^fix/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^bugfix/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^enhancement/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^revert\-\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^migration/\.\*&\#34;,         &\#34;^backport/\.\*&\#34;     \],     &\#34;backport\_rules&\#34;: \[         \{             &\#34;base\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^master$&\#34;,             &\#34;head\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^hotfix/\.\*$&\#34;,             &\#34;backport\_to&\#34;: \[                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^develop$&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: false                 \},                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: true                 \}             \]         \},         \{             &\#34;base\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^master$&\#34;,             &\#34;head\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^release/\.\*$&\#34;,             &\#34;backport\_to&\#34;: \[                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^develop$&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: false                 \},                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: true                 \}             \]         \},         \{             &\#34;base\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^master$&\#34;,             &\#34;head\_trigger\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^migration/\.\*$&\#34;,             &\#34;backport\_to&\#34;: \[                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^develop$&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: false                 \},                 \{                     &\#34;base\_branch\_pattern&\#34;: &\#34;^release/\.\*&\#34;,                     &\#34;only\_active\_pull\_requests&\#34;: true                 \}             \]         \}     \],     &\#34;releasable\_branches&\#34;: \[         &\#34;^master$&\#34;     \],     &\#34;cron\_branches&\#34;: \{         &\#34;version\_branch&\#34;: &\#34;release&\#34;,         &\#34;stable\_branch&\#34;: &\#34;develop&\#34;,         &\#34;target\_branch&\#34;: &\#34;master&\#34;     \} \} </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \* \[Home\]\(/README\.md\) \* \[Overview of tools and pipelines\]\(/overview\.md\) \* Explicabilidad     \* \[Home\]\(explicabilidad/README\.md\)     \* \[Diarizacion\]\(explicabilidad/diarizacion\.md\)

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> This documentation intends to explain how the project is organized and how to contribute to it, being more focused on developer experience\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Marketplace forecasting is a project that aims to forecast the demand of products in the marketplace, providing insights into TGMV and TSI for 9 product categories \+ total for each site\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Git is used to do version control in the repo\. We use the gitflow model to organize our branches, which is a simple convention to how we name our branches and how we merge them\. The main branch is called "master", and the development branch is called "develop"\. We also have a "release" branch, which is used to test the code before merging it to master\. The "hotfix" branch is used to fix bugs in the master branch\. The "feature" branch is used to develop new features and merge them to develop\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: For example, the process of adding a code and would be the following:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: 1\. Create a branch from \`develop\` called \`feature/your\_functionality\`, where \_your\_funcionality\_ should be replaced with a name coherent with the changes being made\. If the code is fixing something, the prefix should be \`fix/\`\. To create a branch from develop, first, checkout to develop

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh git checkout develop git pull </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: And create the new branch

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh git checkout \-b feature/your\_functionality </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Add the changes, commit, and push them to your branch:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh git push </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Then, create a pull request in the project, and add your team and tech lead as reviewers\. Then, merge the code\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: 2\. Create a release branch and merge it with master\. To do this, checkout to develop after the pull request being approved\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  git checkout develop git pull  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Then create the release branch, upgrading the version from the previous release \(you can ask your team or check the list of release/ branches in the github interfaces\)

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \(Replace X\.Y\.Z with the proper version\)

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh git checkout \-b release/X\.Y\.Z git push \-\-set\-upstream release/X\.Y\.Z </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: And do a PR from the github interface\. Thanks to the release process created by RetailCorp, the approval will be automatic since you\'re merging from a release branch\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> We haven\'t included above the steps to create the retail images\. In this flow of feature \-\> develop \-\> release \-\> master branches, we can also create task\-images to test the changes before deploying them to production\. We will explain it in the RDF section

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Poetry is used to manage the dependencies of the project\. We use it to keep track of python library dependencies and to install local environments\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Poetry uses the \`pyproject\.toml\` to identify which packages and versions it should install\. Them, it saves all the info in a \`poetry\.lock\` file\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To install the current project and create a poetry env, run

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  poetry install </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: on the terminal\. You should see that it has created a customized python environment for you\. To run commands with this python, you can do \`poetry run python \-\-version\`, for example

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To add or update packages, we can use the \`poetry add package\` command on the terminal\. The package argument accepts some constraints specifications on the package version, for example \`pandas@latest\`, \`"pandas\>\=2\.0"\`, \`pandas<2\.0\`\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  poetry add pandas@latest poetry add "pandas\>\=2\.0" poetry add "pandas<2\.0"  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> \[\!WARNING\]

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \>When using those constraints, try to always use " " before and after the package specificacion because the terminal can mistake it with some special characters\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Avoid changing the versions in the \`pyproject\.toml\` file directly, because when we do \`poetry add\`, poetry automatically takes care of the dependencies inter\-dependencies\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Poetry has some problems but it is indeed an awesome library, because it helps managing the project and installs \*\*your project as a package in a python environment\*\*\. If you run \`poetry install\`, then you can import the modules inside the \`src/\` folder as you would for a normal package\. In the case of marketplace project:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>python from marketplace\.pipes\.main import pipelines </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Notice that app\.pipes\.main is a module inside this project src/\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: It is important to always keep your python updated with Poetry, so that we ensure all team members are using the same environment\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> \[\!WARNING\]

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> If you have multiple directories inside the \`src/\` directory, don\'t forget adding them to pyproject\.toml, otherwise poetry will not recognize\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \> To do it, add them in the packages section, like so:  \`packages \= \[\{from\="src", include\="app"\}\]\`

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Retail Data Framework is used to build and deploy the project\. It\'s a tool that wraps docker and kubernetes, and manages all projects here at RetailCorp\. More specifically, we will use the Retail Data Application\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Its the core of all data science projects here at RetailCorp\. With RDA, we can create projects, a git repository connected to it, jupyter labs, \_task\-images\_, \_tasks\_, \_workflows\_ and \_agendas\_\. Those last four are the most relevant\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \- \*\*Task\-images\*\* are frozen images of the code at the moment of creation \- they point to a specific commit in the git repository\. They are used to run tasks\. The task\-images are also defined by their entrypoint file, which will be executed later, and a set of input fields \(artifacts and/or parameters\) and outputs \(artifacts\)\. Those inputs are passed to the file through a library called rda\-toolkit, developed by a specialized team at RetailCorp\. We defined those tasks in a filed called \*\*\*tasks\.yml\*\*\*\. This is the place to go to start reading the source code of the project\. \- \*\*Tasks\*\* are the execution of a task\-image\. They are defined by a set of inputs and outputs, which are passed at the moment of execution\. \- \*\*Workflows\*\* are a set of tasks that are executed sequentially\. They are defined by the tasks\-images that will be called, and their interdependencies\. The workflows are defined in \`workflows\.yml\`\. \- \*\*Agendas\*\*: agendas are regular executions of workflows or task\-images\. They are defined by a cron expression, and the workflow or task\-image that will be executed\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: It\'s a good practice to separate the tables that we use for development purposes from the tables used in production\. This help us test changes without affecting the forecasts we send to our internal clients\. To do this, we create 3 environments \(made by rules in the code that change which tables/artifacts we used\): Development \(DEV\), Staging \(HML\) and  Production \(PRD\)\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid  flowchart TD     dev\(DEV environment, in your computer or lab\)     hml\(HML environment, a clone of PRD but targeting other tables and using images tagged with hml\)     prd\(PRD environment, using images tagged with prd\)      dev \-\-\> hml \-\-\> prd  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: We separate the production environment from development and staging using the tags in retail task\-images, and parameters in our code\. The tag \_dev\_ is used for development and testing, \_hml\_ for staging, and \_prd\_ for production\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To create tasks, you should add its specifications to \`tasks\.yml\` file, and then run \`rda prepare\`:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh rda prepare my\_task\_in\_tasks\_yml \-\-version 0\.0\.1 </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: We can also add tags to the version\. Indeed, we use it to differentiate development \(DEV\), staging \(HML\) and production \(PRD\) environments\. If you are creating a package to be used locally, for some rapid tests, you can normally do the command above or add the \_dev\_ tag\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  rda prepare my\_task\_in\_tasks\_yml \-\-version 0\.0\.1\-dev </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: If you want to test that code in a HML environment, do:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  rda prepare my\_tasks\_in\_tasks\_yml \-\-version 0\.0\.1\-hml </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Finally, to put it into production

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  rda prepare my\_tasks\_in\_tasks\_yml \-\-version 0\.0\.1\-prd </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: "How does this tagging help us?" You may be asking\. Well, with it, we can create to isolated environments "HML" and "PRD", and push the changes to affect "HML" before doing it to PRD\. When defining the workflow in \`workflows\.yml\`, we can specify the task version and tags\. So, we will have two different workflows, one aiming at the staging \(HML\) task images and other to the production task images, that can \(and maybe should\) be always lagged with respected to the HML images\. Let\'s dig into the workflows\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Workflows are specified in the workflows\.yml file\. They are defined by a set of tasks, and their interdependencies\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: The usage of workflows follows three steps:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid  flowchart LR     workflow\(Workflow definition \\n in workflows\.yml\)     plan\(Plan\)     execution\(Execution\)      workflow \-\-\>\|rda workflow prepare\| plan \-\-\> \|rda workflow run\| execution  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: One amazing feature of workflows  is the ability to parametrize them\. For example, check this workflow with only one task inside:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>yml workflows\.yml  monitoring\_workflow:     description: "Workflow that monitors the data"     operations:         monitor\_data:             description: "Operation to monitor data"             task\-image:                 name: "monitoring\_pipeline"                 version: "\>\=0\.0\.1, \{\{ ENV \}\}"                 inputs:                     runtime:                          env: "\{\{ ENV \}\}"                         processing\_date: "\{\{ WORKFLOW\.TODAY\_DATE \}\}"                         ref\_date: "\{\{ WORKFLOW\.TODAY\_DATE \- 7 \}\}"           </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: This workflow has some variables inside \`\{\{ \}\}\`\. They serve to parametrize the workflow\. The \`WORKFLOW\.TODAY\_DATE\` is a special parameter provided automatically by Retail Data Framework, and it is replaced by the date value \(YYYY\.M\.D\) at the moment of creation of the workflow plan\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: On the other hand, the \`ENV\` is a parameter that we define when we run the workflow\. We can actually parametrize whatever we want, given that we provide a value at preparation time\. For example:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>sh  rda workflow prepare monitoring\_workflow \-\-version 0\.0\.1 \-\-user\-param ENV:DEV </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Now, check the version parameter in the workflow above\. It specifies that this operation  uses the image \`monitoring\_pipeline\` with version "\>\=0\.0\.1, \{\{ ENV \}\}"\` which, by running with ENV:DEV, is interpreted as "\>\=0\.0\.1, DEV"\. This indicates that it will use the most recent image satisfying version  \>\=0\.0\.1 and having the tag "DEV"\. This is a very powerful feature, because it allows us to have different versions of the same task\-image, and to run them in different environments \(DEV, HML, PRD\)\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Now, let\'s understand the macro picture of the data pipeline\. The data pipeline is divided into modules that are independent of each other, and each is responsible to a particular aspect of the data pipeline\. We represent the data pipeline as a flowchat below:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid flowchart LR          subgraph Flow\["Parametrized by site\_id and target \(tgmv\_lc or tsi\)"\]         direction LR                  subgraph Ingest\["Ingest"\]             direction RL                          i1\[Ingest Timeseries\]             i2\[Ingest holidays\]         end         subgraph Preprocess             direction RL                          i3\[Preprocess timeseries\]             i4\[Preprocess exogenous variables\]          end         subgraph Forecast             direction BT             hyperparams\[Ingest hyperparameters\]             i5\[Run model\]                          hyperparams \-\-\> i5          end          subgraph Postprocess             direction RL             i6\[Prepare data for Stakeholders\]                      end     end      style Flow fill:\#b3cdd1,stroke\-width:0px        Ingest \-\-\> Preprocess \-\-\> Forecast \-\-\> Postprocess </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: The \`Ingest\` module is responsible for ingesting the timeseries and holidays data\. The \`Preprocess\` module is responsible for preprocessing the timeseries and exogenous variables, by filling in missing dates and putting it into a dataframe in the right format for forecasting\. The \`Forecast\` module is responsible for running the model and ingesting the hyperparameters\. The \`Postprocess\` module is responsible for preparing the data for stakeholders, by creating the tables in the format we have agreed with them\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Each module is in a directory of our project\. The ingest classes can be found at \`src/app/pipes/ingest\`\. The preprocess classes can be found at \`src/app/pipes/preprocessing\`\. The forecast classes can be found at \`src/app/pipes/forecasting\`\. The postprocess classes can be found at \`src/app/pipes/postprocessing\`\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: First, be sure that you have installed the project with poetry\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \*\*Ingesting data\*\*

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To ingest data, it\'s really simple\. In your notebook, import the ingest pipe, pass the parameters and run\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>python  from marketplace\.pipes\.ingest\.ingest\_timeseries import IngestMarketplaceData pipe \= IngestMarketplaceData\(site\_id\="RAB", target\="tsi"\) pipe\.run\(\) pipe\.catalog\.load\("series"\) </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Another way to run it is by passing the parameters through a context:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>python  from retailpipes import Context from marketplace\.pipes\.ingest\.ingest\_timeseries import IngestMarketplaceData pipe \= IngestMarketplaceData\(\)  \# With context, we can reuse the same object to ingest different datasets with Context\(site\_id\="RAB", target\="tsi"\):     pipe\.run\(\)     display\(pipe\.catalog\.load\("series"\)\)  with Context\(site\_id\="RAC", target\="tgmv\_lc"\):     pipe\.run\(\)     display\(pipe\.catalog\.load\("series"\)\)  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \*\*Ingest holidays data\*\*

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Similarly, to load holiday data, you can use the HolidaysDatasetPipe:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>python  from marketplace\.pipes\.ingest\.holidays\_and\_campaigns import HolidaysDatasetPipe pipe \= HolidaysDatasetPipe\(site\_id\="RAB", forecast\="MKP\_site"\) pipe\.run\(\) pipe\.catalog\.load\("holidays\_df"\), pipe\.catalog\.load\("campaign\_dummies"\)  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \*\*Run the forecasting\*\*

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To run the forecasting, we have to use other pipelines together with the ingestion steps\. We will use \`PreprocessExogenous\` and \`PreprocessTarget\`\. These steps will prepare the data to be used in the forecasting pipeline\. The forecasting pipeline is \`ProphetVerticalIniciativaPipeline\`\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: We also pass some other parameters to the context\. The forecast horizon parameter "fh" is used both for preprocessing and forecasting\. The processing date parameter "processing\_date" is used for preprocessing\. The method parameter is used for forecasting, and can be "td\_fcst" for Forecast Proportions reconciliaciion, "bu" for Bottom Up and "td\_share" for a naive Topdown\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>python  from retailpipes import Context from marketplace\.pipes\.ingest import HolidaysDatasetPipe, IngestMarketplaceData from marketplace\.pipes\.forecasting\.vertical\_iniciativa\_pipeline import \(     ProphetVerticalIniciativaPipeline, \) from marketplace\.pipes\.preprocessing import PreprocessExogenous, PreprocessTarget  pipe \=  \(     IngestMarketplaceData\(\)      \+ HolidaysDatasetPipe\(\)      \+ PreprocessExogenous\(\)      \+ PreprocessTarget\(\)     \+ ProphetVerticalIniciativaPipeline\(\)     \)   with Context\(          site\_id\="RAB",          target\="tsi",          processing\_date\="2023\-10\-20",          fh\=100,          method\="bu"\):     pipe\.run\(\)  preds \= pipe\.catalog\.load\("preds"\)  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: If you take a look at the \`src/app/pipes/main\.py\`, you will see a dictionary of pipelines mapping names to pipeline classes and the creation of a catalog\. This catalog is a mapping between names and dataset classes\. The names are outputs or inputs of steps in the pipelines \(the green boxes in the figures above\)\. Those are the key ingredients here\. Let\'s understand how they talk with retail and each other\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: In the Retail Data Framework\'s tasks\.yml file, we have to pass an entrypoint informing which file are we willing to execute\.  On the cloud, retail will do something like \`python <entrypoint file\>\`, executing what is inside it\. We can then access the parameters passed in the task through \`retailtk\.rda2\` library\. Note how cumbersome it would be to have to create a new file for every new parameter or task we have\.\.\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: To overcome it, we have an "orchestrate" function, which, according to the parameter "pipeline\_name" passed in \`tasks\.yml\`, executes a specific pipeline\. Then, we can use a single file for all tasks \(and in some cases the same task\-image\), which simplifies the process of deploying\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: The other key ingredient is the catalog\. The catalog receives a dictionary of dataset names and dataset classes and handle all the saving/loading\. In marketplace, the datasets are defined in a yml file\. You can search the names of the datasets to see which pipelines are generating them in the code\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: In the examples above, we aren\'t passing a catalog to the pipeline, because it is optional\. By default, we save the datasets in memory\. To save on bigquery, you should do:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: A deeper dive in how retailpipes is used with Retail Data Framework
content: <code\_tkn\>python  from retailpipes\.rda\.datasets import BigQueryTable from marketplace\.pipes\.main import catalog from retailpipes import Context, Pipeline from marketplace\.pipes\.ingest import HolidaysDatasetPipe, IngestMarketplaceData from marketplace\.pipes\.forecasting\.vertical\_iniciativa\_pipeline import \(     ProphetVerticalIniciativaPipeline, \) from marketplace\.pipes\.preprocessing import PreprocessExogenous, PreprocessTarget  pipe \=  \(      IngestMarketplaceData\(catalog\=catalog\)      \+ HolidaysDatasetPipe\(\)      \+ PreprocessExogenous\(\)      \+ PreprocessTarget\(\)     \+ ProphetVerticalIniciativaPipeline\(\)     \)   with Context\(          site\_id\="RAB",          target\="tsi",          processing\_date\="2023\-10\-20",          fh\=100,          method\="bu"\):     pipe\.run\(\)  </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: This section explains the dailyization of monthly exogenous variables\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $N$ : number of timestamps

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $M$ : number of exogenous features

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $y\(t\)$ : timeseries real values at time $t$

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $y\_j\(t\)$ : timeseries real values at time $t$ for the $j$\-th series \($j \\in \\\{1, \\ldots, N\\\}$\)

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $\\tilde\{y\}\(t\)$ : forecasts for $y\(t\)$, before reconciliation

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $\\hat\{y\}\(t\)$ : forecasts for $y\(t\)$, after reconciliation

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $X$ : matrix of exogenous variables

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $x\_i$ : exogenous variable $i$

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $X\(t\)$ : exogenous variables at time $t$

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: $x\_i\(t\)$ : exogenous variable $i$ at time $t$

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Although we use daily data, for some future exogenous variables we only have information at a monthly level\. In order to use them in our models, we need to disaggregate them to a daily level\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: <code\_tkn\>mermaid graph TD     A\[Month\] \-\-\> B\[Day 1\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> C\[Day 2\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> D\[Day 3\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> AC\[\.\.\.\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> AD\[Day 29\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> AE\[Day 30\]     A\[Month\] \-\-\> AF\[Day 31\]   </code\_tkn\>

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: Time\-hierarchies can be seen as standard hierarchies\. This can be easily visualized by the linear constraint of having the sum of daily values equal to the monthly value\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \\sum\\limits\_\{t \\in month\} x\_i\(t\) \= x\_i\(t\\in month\)

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: where $month$ is the set of days in the month\. and $x\_i\(t\\in month\)$ is the value of the exogenous variable $i$ at that month\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: This is not different of what we do in the hierarchical forecasting, where the sum of bottom levels must be equal to the total level forecast\. Hence, we can use forecast reconciliation strategies to disaggregate the monthly values to a daily level\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: In our particular case, we do not want just to reconcile \- we want to reconcile and keep the total level \(month level\) input exactly before\. The reconciliation should treat the month level as correct\. There are two reconcilation strategies that accomplish this: top\-down and forecast proportions\. The problem of topdown is that it is not clear how it could be applied to this temporal hierarchy since months have different lengths, and Forecast Proportions seems to be the best option\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: The approach is to use a base forecast $\\tilde\{x\}\_i$ of the exogenous variable for future dates, and re\-scale the it so that the total of the month is the given total value $x\_i\(t\\in month\)$\. The base total forecast for the month is

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \\tilde\{x\}\_\{i\}\(t \\in month\) \= \\sum\\limits\_\{t \\in month\} \\tilde\{x\}\_i\(t\) \\cdot

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: To reconcile the daily forecasts, we multiply them by the ratio of $x\_i\(t\\in month\)$ and $\\tilde\{x\}\_i\(t \\in month\)$:

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: \\hat\{x\}\_i\(t\) \= \\tilde\{x\}\_i\(t\) \\cdot \\frac\{x\_i\(t\\in month\)\}\{\\tilde\{x\}\_i\(t \\in month\)\}

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: This reconciled forecast can then be used as an input for the model\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: As a toy example, suppose that we receive from the responsible team that it will be invested 300 dollars in some mechanism such as rebates\. In other words, $x\_i\(t \\in month\) \= 300$\. If our base forecasts for total of the month is 400 dollars \($\\tilde\{x\}\_i\(t \\in month\) \= 400$\), we should re\-scale them to that the total is 300 dollars, while using these base forecasts to compute the share of each day in the month\. This could be achieved by multiplying the base forecasts of the given month by $300/400 \= 0\.75$\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
content: The implementation can be found \[in this file\]\(https://github\.com/retailcorpsrc/retail\_demand\-forecast/blob/6a6612004f7a329935f791777bf9b428cd307eac/src/marketplace/sktime/transformations/temporal\_forecast\_proportions\.py\#L4\)\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: Welcome to marketplace forecasting technical documentation\!
content: This documentation is the repository of explanations for methodologies used in the marketplace forecasting project\. The public of this documentation is the team of data scientists that work on the project\. However, if you are a PL, PM, or any other role that is interested in the project, you are welcome to read it and ask questions\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: Welcome to marketplace forecasting technical documentation\!
How to contribute to this documentation
content: First, check the \[Retail Data Framework Documentation Service Guide\]\(https://retaildocs\.io/documentation\-service/latest/guide/\#/\)\. It is important to understand how the documentation is built and how to contribute to it\.

Name: demand-forecast-docs
section: Welcome to marketplace forecasting technical documentation\!
How to contribute to this documentation
content: \- Set your local machine according to \[this instructions\]\(https://docs\.google\.com/document/d/1VB35N1\_txqnNN5CuYbE5CJTeRSi9fgXg8DGfw38iGsN/edit\)\. It is important to install Retail Data Framework CLI to be able to create new doc versions\! \- Clone \`retail\_demand\-forecast\-docs\` repository \- Create a new branch from \`main\` with the name of the feature you are working on \- Make your changes, commit and create a Pull Request to \`main\` \- Ask for review of one member, and merge after approval\. \- Run \`retail create\-version A\.B\.C\` where \`A\.B\.C\` is the version of your doc\. You can see the \[latest version here\]\(https://web\.retailcloud\.io/demand\-forecast\-docs/versions\)

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This error can be generated by any really rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item could not be restocked correctly and determine the reason for this\. 2\. Resolve the issue\. 3\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock publications and this publication has negative quantity

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This case does not have a specific process defined as it is a very rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item is associated with the negative restock attempt\. 2\. Notify to \*\*Envio Pack\*\* that the restock could not be completed correctly\. 3\. Proceed to identify what happened \(why a restock is executed that leaves the available quantities below 0\)\. 4\. Resolve the issue\.  5\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

content: It happens when something goes wrong while trying to sell a publication without stock into 3PL facilities

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: \[LOW PRIORITY\] Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse&\#39;s stock
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. You should review the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs with the \*\*stream\-prod\*\* scope to easily identify the item and SKU associated with the potential out\-of\-stock sale\. 2\. You should confirm that the item was correctly paused by \*\*checking the item&\#39;s status\*\*\. You need to use api core item endpoint with item\_id and check &\#34;status&\#34; If the item was not correctly paused, you should raise a ticket in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\. 3\. Additionally, you should identify the item to monitor it in case of future out\-of\-stock sales\.

content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to update the quantity of stock into the publication

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs the item whose status could not be updated and determine why it could not be done \(e\.g\., due to a status that does not allow updating the item such as under\\\_review, deleted\.\.\.\)\. 2\. If the item has available units and the status allows sales without stock \(any status other than deleted\), a ticket should be raised in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: Stockkeeper\-api is the application responsible for updating the stock of items belonging to the Standard area that are offered on TechZone\. There are two main endpoints to perform restocking: one for 3PL stock movements \(smaller quantities\) and another for inbound movements, which can contain larger quantities for updating\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Before executing any action on the inventory flows, it is crucial to ensure that both the inbound and outbound

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: movement contain all their test items\. This check is fundamental to avoid affecting the item API and, therefore, the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: API and, therefore, in the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: There are two main flows where this verification is necessary:

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: \- \*\*Error Prevention\*\*: Lack of test items can lead to inventory errors\. \- \*\*API Integrity\*\*: Ensuring that the data sent to the item API is correct is essential to maintain the integrity of the system\. \- \*\*Avoiding Unwanted Alterations\*\*: Incorrect movement can directly affect the inventory status, leading to discrepancies and operational problems\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: 1\. \*\*Review the Inbound \(Restock flow\)\*\*: Make sure that all test items are present\. 2\. \*\*Check the Operation Id \(Feed flow\)\*\*: Confirm that the operationId also contains all items required\. 3\. \*\*Final Validation\*\*: Before proceeding with any action, perform a final validation of both flows\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Implementing this systematic verification is key to maintaining the integrity of the inventory and item API\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Be sure to follow these steps before executing any action on inventory flows\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This error can be generated by any really rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item could not be restocked correctly and determine the reason for this\. 2\. Resolve the issue\. 3\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock publications and this publication has negative quantity

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This case does not have a specific process defined as it is a very rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item is associated with the negative restock attempt\. 2\. Notify to \*\*Envio Pack\*\* that the restock could not be completed correctly\. 3\. Proceed to identify what happened \(why a restock is executed that leaves the available quantities below 0\)\. 4\. Resolve the issue\.  5\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse\'s stock
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to sell a publication without stocK into 3PL facilities

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse\'s stock
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. You should review the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs with the \*\*stream\-prod\*\* scope to easily identify the item and SKU associated with the potential out\-of\-stock sale\. 2\. You should confirm that the item was correctly paused by \*\*checking the item\'s status\*\*\. If the item was not correctly paused, you should raise a ticket in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\. 3\. Additionally, you should identify the item to monitor it in case of future out\-of\-stock sales\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to update the quantity of stock into the publication

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs the item whose status could not be updated and determine why it could not be done \(e\.g\., due to a status that does not allow updating the item such as under\\\_review, deleted\.\.\.\)\. 2\. If the item has available units and the status allows sales without stock \(any status other than deleted\), a ticket should be raised in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: Stockkeeper\-api is the application responsible for updating the stock of items belonging to the Standard area that are offered on TechZone\. There are two main endpoints to perform restocking: one for 3PL stock movements \(smaller quantities\) and another for inbound movements, which can contain larger quantities for updating\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Before executing any action on the inventory flows, it is crucial to ensure that both the inbound and outbound

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: movement contain all their test items\. This check is fundamental to avoid affecting the item API and, therefore, the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: API and, therefore, in the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: There are two main flows where this verification is necessary:

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: \- \*\*Error Prevention\*\*: Lack of test items can lead to inventory errors\. \- \*\*API Integrity\*\*: Ensuring that the data sent to the item API is correct is essential to maintain the integrity of the system\. \- \*\*Avoiding Unwanted Alterations\*\*: Incorrect movement can directly affect the inventory status, leading to discrepancies and operational problems\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: 1\. \*\*Review the Inbound \(Restock flow\)\*\*: Make sure that all test items are present\. 2\. \*\*Check the Operation Id \(Feed flow\)\*\*: Confirm that the operationId also contains all items required\. 3\. \*\*Final Validation\*\*: Before proceeding with any action, perform a final validation of both flows\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Implementing this systematic verification is key to maintaining the integrity of the inventory and item API\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Be sure to follow these steps before executing any action on inventory flows\.

section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This error can be generated by any really rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item could not be restocked correctly and determine the reason for this\. 2\. Resolve the issue\. 3\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to restock publications and this publication has negative quantity

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This case does not have a specific process defined as it is a very rare case that has not been controlled in the code, so it is important to check the logs to identify the problem\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs which item is associated with the negative restock attempt\. 2\. Notify to \*\*Envio Pack\*\* that the restock could not be completed correctly\. 3\. Proceed to identify what happened \(why a restock is executed that leaves the available quantities below 0\)\. 4\. Resolve the issue\.  5\. It may be necessary to perform a manual restock via the database or, in the most extreme case, release a new version\.

content: This alert is to verify that item was correctly paused\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: \[LOW PRIORITY\] Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse\'s stock
content: It happens when something goes wrong while trying to sell a publication without stock into 3PL facilities

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: \[LOW PRIORITY\] Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse\'s stock
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. You should review the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs with the \*\*stream\-prod\*\* scope to easily identify the item and SKU associated with the potential out\-of\-stock sale\. 2\. You should confirm that the item was correctly paused by \*\*checking the item\'s status\*\*\. You need to use api core item endpoint with item\_id and check "status" If the item was not correctly paused, you should raise a ticket in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\. 3\. Additionally, you should identify the item to monitor it in case of future out\-of\-stock sales\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: \[LOW PRIORITY\] Validation stock \(publication has more stock than warehouse\'s stock
content: PS: This alert is just to check item was correctly paused, it may be works automatically

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: This alert happens when something goes wrong while trying to update the quantity of stock into the publication

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: To resolve this alert, you need to do the following: 1\. Identify in the \*\*vendor\-stockkeeper\-api\*\* logs the item whose status could not be updated and determine why it could not be done \(e\.g\., due to a status that does not allow updating the item such as under\\\_review, deleted\.\.\.\)\. 2\. If the item has available units and the status allows sales without stock \(any status other than deleted\), a ticket should be raised in Shield to request the publication be blocked to prevent potential out\-of\-stock sales\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: \[Start\]\(introduction\.md\)

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
content: Stockkeeper\-api is the application responsible for updating the stock of items belonging to the Standard area that are offered on TechZone\. There are two main endpoints to perform restocking: one for 3PL stock movements \(smaller quantities\) and another for inbound movements, which can contain larger quantities for updating\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Before executing any action on the inventory flows, it is crucial to ensure that both the inbound and outbound

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: movement contain all their test items\. This check is fundamental to avoid affecting the item API and, therefore, the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: API and, therefore, in the inventory\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: There are two main flows where this verification is necessary:

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: \- \*\*Error Prevention\*\*: Lack of test items can lead to inventory errors\. \- \*\*API Integrity\*\*: Ensuring that the data sent to the item API is correct is essential to maintain the integrity of the system\. \- \*\*Avoiding Unwanted Alterations\*\*: Incorrect movement can directly affect the inventory status, leading to discrepancies and operational problems\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: 1\. \*\*Review the Inbound \(Restock flow\)\*\*: Make sure that all test items are present\. 2\. \*\*Check the Operation Id \(Feed flow\)\*\*: Confirm that the operationId also contains all items required\. 3\. \*\*Final Validation\*\*: Before proceeding with any action, perform a final validation of both flows\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Implementing this systematic verification is key to maintaining the integrity of the inventory and item API\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Be sure to follow these steps before executing any action on inventory flows\.

Name: vendor-stockkeeper-api
section: Verification of Items in Inventory Flows
content: Before we send any kind of inbound we must first get that inbound and go to inbound conciliation to check if the items have the tags that identify an item as a test item\.
